jokes.text: You accidentally burn down a building, and you get 120 hours of community service?
jokes.setup: Character complaining about consequences for burning down a building
jokes.punchline: Treating arson as a minor accident deserving light punishment
jokes.explanation: Strong opening that establishes the show's dark tone - treating arson casually. Good economy and character-defining moment.

jokes.text: I don't like kids. I don't wanna work with 'em.
jokes.setup: Learning they have to work with kids for community service
jokes.punchline: Immediate, blunt rejection of helping children
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing these people's selfishness and lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: Interstate... Sanitation. Jesus Christ! / That's the guys with the orange vests who pick up trash.
jokes.setup: Reading community service assignment
jokes.punchline: Horror at having to do manual labor picking up trash
jokes.explanation: Shows their privileged attitude and disdain for working-class jobs.

jokes.text: Why would you tell the judge you were drunk? / 'Cause I was drunk. / But why would you say that? / I thought that would get me off the hook.
jokes.setup: Question about confessing to being drunk to a judge
jokes.punchline: The logic that admitting guilt would somehow help
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that reveals character stupidity. Well-structured dialogue exchange.

jokes.text: Those mimosas were like eight bucks a pop, Charlie. / Well, you had like seven of'em.
jokes.setup: Complaining about expensive brunch bill
jokes.punchline: Revealing Charlie drank seven expensive mimosas
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their priorities and Charlie's drinking problem.

jokes.text: You don't have any money? What are you... Are you serious? Ah! Ah!
jokes.setup: Discovering someone has no money for the bill
jokes.punchline: Frustrated screaming at the realization
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe moment, not particularly original but fits the tone.

jokes.text: Asians love gambling.
jokes.setup: Explaining Asian people at the bar
jokes.punchline: Casual racist stereotype delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Shows character awfulness but relies on lazy stereotyping. The joke is that they'd say this, not the stereotype itself.

jokes.text: You were in Vietnam? / Don't get excited, Mac... He was in Vietnam 10 years ago on a business trip.
jokes.setup: Impressive claim about being in Vietnam
jokes.punchline: Deflation - it was just a recent business trip
jokes.explanation: Well-executed misdirection with good setup and payoff. Classic Sunny deflation of masculine posturing.

jokes.text: If you don't go, me and Mac and Charlie, we're gonna sell our portion of the bar. / No, we're not. / No, that's not happening. / Just back me up on this. / No, I can't really back you up.
jokes.setup: Trying to make a power play threat
jokes.punchline: Immediate abandonment by supposed allies
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment showing the gang's inability to work together and support each other.

jokes.text: I'm not an alcoholic actually. Yeah, I'm only here 'cause I got a little drunk and I threw a flaming bag of feces into a building and I burnt it down a little bit.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to explain he's not an alcoholic at AA meeting
jokes.punchline: Casually describing arson with feces as 'a little' incident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment. The casual delivery of increasingly horrific details while claiming innocence is perfect Charlie. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I wasn't trying to burn it down. I was trying to make the place smell real bad and get rid of this guy.
jokes.setup: Explaining his arson wasn't intentional
jokes.punchline: The 'logical' plan was to throw flaming feces for smell-based eviction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - the plan was somehow worse than the accident. Brilliant character-specific absurdity.

jokes.text: He was drinking a beer when he came in.
jokes.setup: Charlie denying he's an alcoholic at AA
jokes.punchline: Someone pointing out he arrived drunk to the meeting
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy that undercuts Charlie's denial with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Always a bunch of clowns.
jokes.setup: Community service workers arriving
jokes.punchline: Dismissive assessment of court-ordered volunteers
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment establishing the youth center worker's attitude toward the gang.

jokes.text: All right. Now the rest of you kids can go with those two losers right there.
jokes.setup: Dividing kids into basketball teams
jokes.punchline: Openly insulting the other coaches in front of children
jokes.explanation: Shows the character's inappropriate behavior around children and lack of professionalism.

jokes.text: Are you kidding me? Are... You guys are the Wildcats? Jesus Christ! Is anyone absent? Anyone tall? What are those, flip-flops?
jokes.setup: Dennis seeing his assigned team
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire complaints about every aspect of his players
jokes.explanation: Good escalating frustration that shows Dennis's perfectionism and impatience with children.

jokes.text: Jesus, kid. Did you not wear shoes? You don't know? Did anyone else not bring shoes?
jokes.setup: Kid wearing flip-flops to basketball
jokes.punchline: Dennis's incredulous reaction to basic oversight
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's unrealistic expectations and poor understanding of working with children.

jokes.text: Yeah, it could be a guy who's never participated in any kind of sporting event ever. / Yeah, or it could be a girl. That's enough said, right?
jokes.setup: Arguing over who should be head coach
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual sexism as his argument
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate sexism that shows Mac's backwards attitudes, though not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Terrible. Take a lap. / God awful. Get goin'.
jokes.setup: Kids playing basketball
jokes.punchline: Mac berating children like a drill sergeant
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's inappropriate intensity with children and lack of understanding about youth sports.

jokes.text: Do I look like I need to be on a diet? / I don't know. / Terrible. Take a lap.
jokes.setup: Mac asking kid about diet soda
jokes.punchline: Kid's honest response getting him punished
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Mac's insecurity and inappropriate response to honest feedback.

jokes.text: See, kids, that's 18-0. Fundamentals don't mean shit if you got no strength.
jokes.setup: Mac dominating kids in basketball
jokes.punchline: Swearing while teaching inappropriate lesson to children
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's competitive nature and complete inappropriateness as a youth mentor.

jokes.text: Charlie, are you here to try to have sex with me? / I can't believe you could ask me a question like that in my time of need.
jokes.setup: Waitress suspicious of Charlie's motives
jokes.punchline: Charlie's indignant response despite obvious intentions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - transparent manipulation disguised as hurt feelings.

jokes.text: maybe I'll just get a little comfortable and I won't talk, and you can just kinda... / Just watch your feet on the... / I'll take my shoes off. / No. Just don't take your shoes off.
jokes.setup: Charlie making himself at home in Waitress's apartment
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly desperate attempts to maintain boundaries
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Charlie's boundary issues and the Waitress's discomfort.

jokes.text: You ever... You ever owned a dog? Do you want a dog? This thing is cozy.
jokes.setup: Charlie getting comfortable on Waitress's bed
jokes.punchline: Random personal questions while making himself at home
jokes.explanation: Shows Charlie's obliviousness to social cues and inappropriate intimacy assumptions.

jokes.text: Quite frankly, I don't see what all the fuss is about. I banged her. I don't get it.
jokes.setup: Frank asking about Charlie's obsession with the Waitress
jokes.punchline: Dennis casually revealing he slept with her with no understanding of why that's problematic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment showing his narcissism and lack of empathy for Charlie.

jokes.text: Dennis, you shouldn't be sleeping with your friends' girlfriends.
jokes.setup: Dennis revealing he slept with the Waitress
jokes.punchline: Frank giving surprisingly reasonable relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Ironic moment where Frank is the voice of reason, subverting expectations.

jokes.text: Elbows. Knees. Say it with me once. Ready? Elbows. Knees.
jokes.setup: Mac coaching basketball fundamentals
jokes.punchline: Teaching kids to use elbows and knees as weapons
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Mac teaching violence to children

jokes.text: Don't get all high-and-mighty because you got a pantsuit. You look like Big Bird!
jokes.setup: Arguing with female coach about authority
jokes.punchline: Mac's ridiculous insult comparing her to Sesame Street character
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly specific and childish insult that's perfectly Mac-like in its randomness.

jokes.text: Whoo! Whoo! That's it! Gambling!
jokes.setup: Everyone making increasingly high bets on the basketball game
jokes.punchline: Frank's enthusiastic celebration of the gambling escalation
jokes.explanation: Frank's pure joy at corrupting the youth basketball league with gambling.

jokes.text: I want everybody to insert their safety pin into their wristband sharp side out. All right, if you come down right on the top part and smash down with all your weight you'll break the kid's foot in three places.
jokes.setup: Mac giving basketball instruction to children
jokes.punchline: Teaching kids how to injure opponents with concealed weapons
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation of Mac's inappropriate coaching. The specific detail about breaking feet 'in three places' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: What if I get hurt? / That's a good question. Now as long as you hurt the other kid as bad or worse than he hurts you you'll have done your job, and I'll be proud of you.
jokes.setup: Kid asking about getting injured
jokes.punchline: Mac encouraging mutual harm as a coaching philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the violence theme with Mac's twisted parental approval system.

jokes.text: Lesson number one: Winnin' ain't everything. Lesson two: Do you own a bike?
jokes.setup: Frank giving apparently wholesome life lessons to star player
jokes.punchline: Immediate pivot to asking about a bike (implying bribery)
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - starts like genuine wisdom then reveals corruption. Perfect economy and character work.

jokes.text: I only offered to be your sponsor so I could get closer to your friend Dennis.
jokes.setup: Waitress has been helping Charlie with sobriety
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was all manipulation to get to Dennis
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - the one person helping Charlie was also using him. Shows everyone in this universe is selfish.

jokes.text: You gotta... 'Cause what if I start drinking again? / That's a risk I'm willing to take.
jokes.setup: Charlie worried about relapse without sponsor
jokes.punchline: Waitress's callous dismissal of his sobriety
jokes.explanation: Brutally efficient line showing the Waitress's selfishness. Perfect economy and dark humor.

jokes.text: All right. These are for you. And there's a steel toe in there. Don't be afraid to use it.
jokes.setup: Frank giving equipment to a player
jokes.punchline: The 'equipment' is weaponized shoes for hurting opponents
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the violence theme. Frank fully embracing corruption of youth sports.

jokes.text: Hey, Chris, you in there? Chris, come on, buddy. Let's go. / What the hell are you doing? The game's about to start. / I got a new bike.
jokes.setup: Dennis looking for his star player before the big game
jokes.punchline: Kid revealing he was bribed with a bike
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the bike setup. Shows the corruption has worked and Dennis's plan is ruined.

jokes.text: Charlie, what are you doing? / I volunteered. / Let's play some ball. / Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Charlie showing up drunk to referee the kids' game
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic arrival while clearly intoxicated
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Charlie followed through on his drinking threat and is now corrupting the game.

jokes.text: Yeah, I am drunk. You know why? I don't have a sponsor anymore!
jokes.setup: Charlie being confronted about being drunk
jokes.punchline: His indignant justification for drinking
jokes.explanation: Charlie's logic for drinking - losing the sponsor gave him permission to drink again.

jokes.text: You call this game! This game is over! / You got a problem? Button that lip! / Don't tell me to button my lip! / Is that a threat? / Yeah, that's a threat!
jokes.setup: Adults arguing during children's basketball game
jokes.punchline: Drunk Charlie escalating to threats at youth sports
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how the adults have completely corrupted what should be innocent children's sports.

jokes.text: Get out of here! You're in the showers!
jokes.setup: Charlie as drunk referee ejecting someone from the game
jokes.punchline: Using inappropriate sports terminology for ejection
jokes.explanation: Charlie's drunk attempt at official referee language comes out wrong but shows his commitment to the bit.
